This page introduces a manual for Oracle FLEXCUBE's Common Core Electronic Messaging Service module and outlines its intended audience, accessibility information, organization, and the content of subsequent chapters.
pages.raw_text:
1-1
1. Preface
1.1 
Introduction
This manual is designed to help acquaint you with the Common Core Electronic Messaging 
Service module of Oracle FLEXCUBE.
The module supports various types of media. Besides standard media modes like Mail, Telex 
and SWIFT you can also use other media types supported by the Media Control System 
(MCS) installed at the node at which the database of your branch is installed. Oracle 
FLEXCUBE gives native support for media like Mail, Telex, and SWIFT using EMS as the 
MCS.
You can further obtain information specific to a particular field by placing the cursor on the 
relevant field and striking <F1> on the keyboard.
1.2 
Audience
This manual is intended for the following User/User Roles:
1.3 
Documentation Accessibility
For information about Oracle's commitment to accessibility, visit the Oracle Accessibility 
Program website at http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=docacc.
1.4 
Organization
This manual is organized as follows:
Role
Function
Back office data entry clerk
Input functions for contracts
Back office managers/officers
Authorization functions
Product Managers
Product definition and authorization
End of Day operators
Processing during End of Day/Beginning of Day
Financial Controller/Product Manag-
ers
Generation of reports
Chapter
Description
Chapter 1
Preface gives information on the intended audience. It also lists the 
various chapters covered in this User Manual.
Chapter 2
EMS Maintenances explains the maintenance of various Messaging 
functions.
Chapter 3
Function ID Glossary has alphabetical listing of Function/Screen ID's 
used in the module with page references for quick navigation.

pages.raw_text:
1.5 
Glossary of Icons
This User Manual may refer to all or some of the following icons.
1.6 
Related Documents
For further information on procedures discussed in the manual, refer to the Oracle 
FLEXCUBE manuals on:
пБђпА†Common Procedures
пБђпА†Accessibility
Icons
Function
Exit
Add row
Delete row
Option List

pages.summary: This page discusses maintenance procedures for EMS and introduces Message Media Control Maintenance, explaining its role in managing external media control systems used for transmitting messages to and from a bank.
pages.raw_text:
2-1
2. EMS Maintenances
2.1 
Introduction
This chapter contains the following sections:
пБђпА†Section 2.2, "Message Media Control Maintenance"
пБђпА†Section 2.3, "Media Maintenance"
пБђпА†Section 2.4, "Message Queues Mapping Maintenance"
пБђпА†Section 2.5, "Message Queue Maintenance"
пБђпА†Section 2.6, "SWIFT Message Notification Browser"
пБђпА†Section 2.7, "SWIFT Net Service Definition"
пБђпА†Section 2.8, "SWIFT Local Authentication Maintenance"
пБђпА†Section 2.9, "Mapping Rule Group"
пБђпА†Section 2.10, "Defining Rule Group"
пБђпА†Section 2.11, "Routing Rule Definition"
пБђпА†Section 2.12, "Defining Protocol Parameter"
пБђпА†Section 2.13, "File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration"
пБђпА†Section 2.14, "Distinguished Name Definition"
пБђпА†Section 2.15, "Defining Debulk Rule"
пБђпА†Section 2.16, "Connectivity Operation Manager"
пБђпА†Section 2.17, "Outbound File Browser"
пБђпА†Section 2.18, "Inbound File Browser"
пБђпА†Section 2.19, "Defining Bulk Preference"
2.2 
Message Media Control Maintenance
The messages that are sent from and delivered to your bank are transmitted and received 
over sources that are external to Oracle FLEXCUBE. We shall call these external sources 
Media Control Systems (MCS).
In a distributed environment, the database of a branch is located in a node or server. The MCS 
of the messages are also installed in a node. Thus, while defining an MCS, you also need to 
indicate the node in which it is installed.
An MCS can handle only one media, hence you need to set up several media control systems 
for the various media types maintained for your bank. Apart from indicating the media type for 
an MCS, you can indicate separate directories from which Oracle FLEXCUBE should read 
and write incoming and outgoing messages for a given media.
You can maintain MCS details in the вАШMessage Media Control MaintenanceвАЩ screen. The 
details that you specify in this screen control the medium of delivery and reception of 
messages.
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.2.1, "Maintaining Message Media Control"
пБђпА†Section 2.2.2, "Specifying File Prefix"

pages.summary: This section explains how to maintain message media control details in Oracle FLEXCUBE, including invoking the maintenance screen and specifying delivery types such as Folder or Queue.
pages.raw_text:
2-2
пБђпА†Section 2.2.3, "Saving Message Media Control Details"
2.2.1 
Maintaining Message Media Control
You can invoke вАШMessage Media Control MaintenanceвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDMGMCSвАЩ in the 
field at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow 
button.
If you are maintaining details of a new media control system click the вАШNewвАЩ button on the 
Application toolbar. The вАШMessage Media Control MaintenanceвАЩ screen is displayed without 
any details.
If you are calling an MCS record that has already been defined, double-click a record of your 
choice from the summary screen. In the вАШSummaryвАЩ screen, all the media control system 
records that you have entered will be displayed in a tabular form. 
The screen is as shown below:
You can capture the following details on this screen.
Delivery Type
You can specify the delivery type here. Oracle FLEXCUBE gives you two options viz. Folder 
and Queue. Depending on the selection you make, you must specify the details in the 
corresponding fields as follows.
пБђпА†Folder вАУ If you choose вАШFolderвАЩ as the delivery type, you must specify the In Directory 
and the Out Directory. Further, after selecting вАШFolderвАЩ, if you check the option вАШUnix Swift 
ServerвАЩ for a UNIX SWIFT server, then you must specify the Unix In-Directory and the 
Unix Out-Directory. For a Windows Server the In-Directory and Out-Directory must be 
maintained.
пБђпА†Queue вАУ If you select вАШQueueвАЩ as the delivery type, you must specify In Queue, Out 
Queue and the type of queue вАУ i.e. вАШMicrosoft Message QueueвАЩ or WebSphere 
Messaging QueueвАЩ.

pages.summary: This page explains the setup and configuration of Media Control Systems in Oracle FLEXCUBE, including how to assign codes, specify media types, indicate statuses, and manage directories.
pages.raw_text:
2-3
Media Control System
In Oracle FLEXCUBE, each media control system that you maintain is identified by a 15-
character code called an MCS code. You can follow your own convention for devising this 
code.
The code that you assign an MCS should be unique as it is used to identify the external 
source.
Node
A node is the Database instance on which Oracle FLEXCUBE is installed. On assigning a 
code to an MCS, you can indicate the node or server at which the MCS is located. A branchвАЩs 
database is located in a node and an MCS is also installed in a node. Thus, while defining an 
MCS, you need to indicate the node at which it has been installed.
Media 
You need to also specify the media for which your bank is using the MCS. For example, if you 
set up an MCS say вАШMCSSWIFTвАЩ and indicate the media type as SWIFT, it indicates that 
Oracle FLEXCUBE can receive and transmit SWIFT messages through the MCS 
вАШMCSSWIFTвАЩ.
The following diagram illustrates a typical MCS setup:I
Status
You need to indicate the status of an MCS. The status options available are:
пБђпА†Active and 
пБђпА†Passive
It is only when an MCS is active that messages will be directed to it. If, for instance, an MCS 
located at the node in which your branch operates malfunctions, you can indicate that the 
MCS is passive. In this case, Oracle FLEXCUBE will not write into or read from the directories 
on the node. No message will be routed through a passive MCS.
In Directory and Out Directory
If the Delivery Type is вАШFolder' and the SWIFT server is a Windows server then apart  from 
indicating the node on which an MCS is located, you should specify the full path of the

pages.summary: This page describes settings for configuring Oracle FLEXCUBE to handle incoming and outgoing SWIFT messages, including file prefixes, queue paths, and directory specifications.
pages.raw_text:
2-4
directories from which Oracle FLEXCUBE should read and write incoming and outgoing 
messages respectively.
File Prefix
For the MCS you are maintaining, you should identify the outgoing message files generated 
in a different media with unique prefixes. Enter the unique identifier in this field.  
In Queue
If the Delivery type is Queue then you should enter the full path of the queue in the node or 
server into which the MCS should store the incoming message hand-off file. Oracle 
FLEXCUBE, by default, will pickup and read all incoming messages transmitted through the 
specified media from this queue.
Out Queue
Enter the full path of the queue in the node or server into which the message hand-off file from 
Oracle FLEXCUBE for the specified media should be stored. The MCS, which is also, located 
on the same node, will by default store the outgoing messages in this queue. 
Unix Swift Server
At your bank, if the SWIFT server is on UNIX, check this option. Subsequently, in this screen, 
you should specify the default In and Out Directories for the SWIFT message hand-off files. 
To continue with your normal banking operations you can connect to another node and 
indicate the directory on that node from which Oracle FLEXCUBE should read from and write 
into.
SWIFT Server on UNIX
If the SWIFT server at your bank is on UNIX, you should indicate it in this screen. 
Subsequently, you should specify the default In and Out Directories for the SWIFT message 
hand-off files.
Specifying the UNIX In-Directory 
You can specify the full path of the directory on the SWIFT server where you would like to 
store incoming SWIFT message files. Oracle FLEXCUBE will pickup and process all incoming 
SWIFT message files from this directory.
Specifying the UNIX Out-Directory 
In this screen, you can also specify the directory on the SWIFT server where you would like 
to store outgoing SWIFT message hand-off files. 
2.2.2 
Specifying File Prefix
For the MCS you are maintaining, you should identify outgoing message files generated in the 
media with unique prefixes. Oracle FLEXCUBE will automatically prefix outgoing message 
hand-off files for the MCS with the prefix that you specify.
2.2.3 
Saving Message Media Control Details
After you have made the mandatory entries, save the record. An MCS record that you have 
created should be authorized by a user, bearing a different Login ID, before the End of Day 
process (EOD) is run.
2.3 
Media Maintenance
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.3.1, "Maintaining Message Media Details"

pages.summary: This section explains how to access and use the 'Message Media Maintenance' screen in Oracle FLEXCUBE to manage media types for transmitting messages.
pages.raw_text:
2-5
2.3.1 
Maintaining Message Media Details
You can invoke the вАШMessage Media MaintenanceвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDMEDMTвАЩ in the field 
at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button.
If you are maintaining details of a new media type, select вАШNewвАЩ from the Actions Menu, or click 
new icon on the toolbar. The вАШMessage Media MaintenanceвАЩ screen is displayed without any 
details. 
If you are calling a Message Media Maintenance record that has already been defined, 
double-click a record from the summary screen. In the вАШSummaryвАЩ screen, all the media 
maintenance records that you have entered will be displayed in a tabular form. 
The screen is as shown below:
In this screen, you can maintain the following:
пБђпА†The media types that can be used to transmit messages from and to your bank, and
пБђпА†The compatible media for the media type you are maintaining.
For each media type that you maintain, you should indicate the following parameters:
Indicating a Code to Identify a Media Type
In Oracle FLEXCUBE, each media type that you maintain is identified with a fifteen-character 
code called вАШMedia CodeвАЩ. You can follow your own convention for devising this code. A Media 
Type could be SWIFT, Mail, Telex, ISO etc.
The code that you assign a media type should be unique as it is used to identify the media. 
When you need to indicate that a message should be transmitted through a particular media

sections: Repeating a Set of Padding Characters
pages.summary: This page explains how to configure message markers and padding characters for media types in Oracle FLEXCUBE, including settings for outgoing and incoming messages.
pages.raw_text:
2-6
type, you just need to specify the code assigned to the media type. The message will be 
routed automatically through the media. 
You can enter a short description of the media type that you are maintaining. The description 
will help you identify the code that it represents. 
Opting for Message Markers
You can opt to append end-message markers to outgoing messages generated in a media. 
These markers are referred to as вАЬPadding CharactersвАЭ in Oracle FLEXCUBE. Padding 
characters would, typically, be a standard set of characters, probably repeated. The padding 
characters that you specify will be inserted, automatically, at the end of every outgoing 
message in the media. You can indicate the padding characters that mark the end of an 
outgoing message (in a specific media) in the Message Suffix field.
When defining a media, you can also capture the padding characters that mark the end of 
incoming messages in the media. The system identifies the end of an incoming message, in 
a file containing several messages, when it encounters the padding characters you have 
captured for a media type. Enter the padded characters that mark the end of incoming 
messages in a media in the Message Terminator field.
Note
For Message suffix to be used for Outgoing messages, the вАШPadding RequiredвАЩ option should be se-
lected.
Repeating a Set of Padding Characters
If you opted to suffix an outgoing message with a set of padding characters, indicate in the 
вАШNumber of CharactersвАЩ field the number of times the set should be repeated.
The padding characters will be suffixed to every outgoing message in the media as many 
times as you specify.
Halting the Processing of Messages
At any time, you can opt to halt the processing of messages in a specific media. To halt the 
processing of incoming and outgoing messages in a media, invoke this screen and navigate 
to the maintenance record for the media, and choose the вАШStop ProcessingвАЩ option. When you 
save your changes to the record, Oracle FLEXCUBE will stop processing messages in the 
media.
Indicating the Compatible Media for a Media Type
For each media type that you maintain, you can also indicate other media that are compatible. 
The format of compatible media should be similar.
For example, you are maintaining details for the media type 'mail'. You have indicated media 
type 'Fax' as compatible media.
In this case, whenever a message is faxed, it will be in the mail format defined for the message 
in the вАШAdvice Format File MaintenanceвАЩ screen.
You can indicate compatible media by selecting the relevant media codes from the picklist 
available. To add compatible media for a media type, select the code that identifies the 
compatible media, and click add icon. To delete compatible media, select the codes that 
identify the media, and click delete icon.

pages.summary: This page explains how to set priorities for different media types used in message dispatching and introduces the process of maintaining message queues mapping.
pages.raw_text:
2-7
Note
The compatible media that you indicate for a media type should have already been main-
tained as a media type.
Setting the Priority
While maintaining media types, you can set the usage priority for each media type that you 
maintain. Consequently, when dispatching messages to customers, the media type used for 
sending the message will be the one that is higher on the priority rating. For instance, let us 
assume that the following are the types of media that you bank uses to send advices to 
customers:
пБђпА†SWIFT
пБђпА†Mail
пБђпА†Telex
пБђпА†Fax
пБђпА†ISO
You have assigned the following priority to each of these media types:
When dispatching messages to customers, the system will, by default, select the media type 
in the ascending order of the priority that you have specified. 
Note
The rating that you specify can be anything between 1 and 99. A rating of one would indi-
cate that the usage of the particular media type is highest on the priority list; whereas, a 
rating of 99 indicates that the usage or the particular media type is of lowest priority. 
Saving the Record
After you have made mandatory entries, save the record. A media maintenance record that 
you have created should be authorized by a user, bearing a different Login ID, before the End 
of Day processes are triggered. Click вАШExitвАЩ or вАШCancelвАЩ button to return to the application.
2.4 
Message Queues Mapping Maintenance
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.4.1, "Invoking Message Queue Mapping Maintenance Screen"
Media 
Type
Media 
Priority
SWIFT
2
Mail
1
Telex
4
Fax
3
ISO
5

sections: Invoking Message Queue Mapping Maintenance Screen
pages.summary: This section explains how to invoke the 'Message Queue Mapping Maintenance' screen and the routing criteria for MT700 and MT701 messages.
pages.raw_text:
2-8
2.4.1 
Invoking Message Queue Mapping Maintenance Screen
For a combination of the following, you can specify the branch (queue) to which the message 
is to be routed:
пБђпА†Media вАУ represents the delivery media. Typically, your entry would be SWIFT. 
пБђпА†BIC Code - represents the receiverвАЩs BIC for which a message is to be routed to a 
specific queue in a particular branch. You can choose to specify the wildcard ALL entry 
in this field. 
пБђпА†Message Type - indicates the message type for which routing procedure is required. 
You can select the message type from the available adjoining list.
пБђпА†Currency вАУ indicates the currency of the incoming message. For example in an MT103 
it would be from tag 32A. This is an additional parameter to determine the booking 
branch and queue. You can also choose to maintain the wildcard ALL entry. 
пБђпА†Booking Branch - indicates the branch in which incoming messages are routed. A set 
of all the branches having the same SWIFT BIC as that mentioned in the BIC code field 
is displayed in the option list. 
This maintenance can be performed through the вАШMessage Queue Mapping MaintenanceвАЩ 
screen. To invoke this screen, type вАШMSDQMAPвАЩ in the field at the top right corner of the 
Application tool bar and click the adjoining arrow button.
While processing MT700 and MT701 messages the System ensures the following:
пБђпА†MT700 and MT701 are routed to the branch using the Incoming Routing specifications 
maintained in this screen
пБђпА†For MT700, in case the branch of the corresponding MT701 is different from that of the 
MT700 it is re-aligned to the branch of the MT700
пБђпА†MT701 messages are processed only after the receipt of MT 700
Note
You can maintain the same BIC for the main branch as well as the sub-branch.

pages.summary: This section explains how to maintain message queues for incoming SWIFT and non-SWIFT messages, including setting up unique queue names, descriptions, and routing rules.
pages.raw_text:
2-9
2.5 
Message Queue Maintenance
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.5.1, "Maintaining Message Queues"
2.5.1 
Maintaining Message Queues
All Incoming SWIFT and Non Swift Messages are routed through a messaging queue. You 
need to maintain different user queues to which incoming messages are directed. Users with 
appropriate rights are allowed to access a particular queue. All 798 incoming index messages 
770,761,784 are also captured in this screen. 
You can invoke the вАШMessage Queue MaintenanceвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDQMNTвАЩ in the field 
at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking the adjoining arrow button.
In this screen you can capture the following details for a queue:
пБђпА†A name to identify the queue uniquely throughout the system.
пБђпА†A short description of the queue
пБђпА†Select вАШAuto STPвАЩ to process SWIFT message automatically if it is part of any queue.
пБђпА†The codes of various SWIFT and Non SWIFT messages that would be routed to this 
queue. 
пБђпА†Select collection queue flag if the unique queue you are maintaining here is a collection 
Queue. 
Note
The codes of various SWIFT and Non SWIFT messages list in the grid is not applicable 
for the collection queue.
You can assign a message to more than one messaging queue. At the time of maintaining 
rules for a message (discussed in the subsequent sections of this document), you can select 
the appropriate queue for each rule from the list of queues to which the message is linked.

pages.summary: This section describes how to add and remove messages from a queue and explains the SWIFT Message Notification Browser used for viewing ACK/NACK and delivery notification messages in Oracle FLEXCUBE.
pages.raw_text:
2-10
Select вАШAddвАЩ from the Actions menu in the Application tool bar or click add icon to add a 
message to the queue being defined. To remove a message from the queue, Select вАШDeleteвАЩ 
from the Actions menu in the Application tool bar or click delete icon.
2.6 
SWIFT Message Notification Browser
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.6.1, "Viewing SWIFT Notification Messages"
2.6.1 
Viewing SWIFT Notification Messages
SWIFT provides the following two types of notification messages to Oracle FLEXCUBE:
пБђпА†ACK/NACK acknowledgement messages indicating whether the message has been 
successfully delivered to SWIFTNet FIN service, from where it gets forwarded to the 
receiving system
пБђпА† Delivery notification messages indicating whether the message was successfully 
delivered to the receiving system
You can access these notifications messages using вАШSWIFT Message Notification BrowserвАЩ 
screen. You can invoke this screen by screen by typing вАШMSSNOTIBвАЩ in the field at the top 
right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button. The screen 
is as shown below:
In the above screen, you can base your queries on any or all of the following parameters and 
fetch records:
пБђпА†Document Number
пБђпА†Sender
пБђпА†Swift Message Type
пБђпА†Process Status
пБђпА†Auth Status

pages.summary: This page describes how to perform a query using user and reference parameters, detailing the displayed record information, and introduces sections for invoking and viewing SWIFTNet service definitions.
pages.raw_text:
2-11
пБђпА†User Reference
пБђпА†Reference Number
Select any or all of the above parameters for a query and click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The records 
meeting the selected criteria are displayed.
System displays the following details pertaining to the fetched records:
пБђпА†Branch
пБђпА†Document Number
пБђпА†Sender
пБђпА†Swift Message Type
пБђпА†Branch Date
пБђпА†Maker ID
пБђпА†Release Time
пБђпА†Repair Reason
пБђпА†Running Number
пБђпА†Process Status
пБђпА†Auth Status
пБђпА†Checker DT Stamp
пБђпА†Checker ID
пБђпА†Message Reference
пБђпА†Receiver
пБђпА†User Reference
пБђпА†Mod Number
пБђпА†reference Number
A back-ground job running on this browser updates the outgoing browser with the ACK/NAK 
and the delivery status of the message. The sender MUR from SWIFT is used to match the 
corresponding outgoing browser record.
2.7 
SWIFT Net Service Definition
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.7.1, "Invoking SWIFTNet Service Definition Screen"
пБђпА†Section 2.7.2, "Viewing SWIFTNet Service Summary"

pages.summary: This section explains how to invoke and configure the 'SWIFTNet Service Definition' screen for specifying service details such as name, mode, and DNs.
pages.raw_text:
2-12
2.7.1 
Invoking SWIFTNet Service Definition Screen
You can invoke вАШSWIFTNet Service DefinitionвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDSWTSRвАЩ in the field at 
the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button...
You can specify the following fields:
Service Name
Specify the SWIFTnet Service name.
Service Mode
Select any one of the service modes.
пБђпА†Real Time- Choose this button to send and receive messages real-time. Real Time 
option is selected by default.
пБђпА†Store and Forward - Choose this button to send and receive messages by storing them 
in a location and then forwarding them accordingly.
Request for DN
Specify the DN that send request to the service. You must provide at least one DN.
Responder DN
Specify the DN that responds request to the service. You must provide at least one DN.

pages.summary: This page describes how to view a summary of SWIFTNet services and maintain local authentication for SWIFT messages, including accessing the maintenance screen and processing incoming and outgoing messages.
pages.raw_text:
2-13
2.7.2 
Viewing SWIFTNet Service Summary
You can invoke вАШSWIFTNet Service SummaryвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSSSWTSRвАЩ in the field at 
the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button. 
You can search using one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†Service Name
пБђпА†Authorization Status 
пБђпА†Record Status
Once you have specified the search parameters, click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The system displays 
the records that match the search criteria for the following
пБђпА†Service Name
пБђпА†Authorization Status 
пБђпА†Record Status
2.8 
SWIFT Local Authentication Maintenance
This section contains the following topics:
пБђпА†Section 2.8.1, "Invoking SWIFT LAU Maintenance Screen"
пБђпА†Section 2.8.2, "Viewing SWIFT LAU Summary Details"
пБђпА†Section 2.8.3, "Processing Outgoing SWIFT LAU"
пБђпА†Section 2.8.4, "Processing Incoming SWIFT LAU"
2.8.1 
Invoking SWIFT LAU Maintenance Screen
You can maintain SWIFT authentication parameters in вАШSWIFT LAU MaintenanceвАЩ screen. 
This screen can be accessed from Head Office only. The EMS module refers SWIFT LAU 
maintenance data for calculating checksum for outbound and authentication of inbound 
messages. HMAC-SHA256 algorithm validation will be used to calculate the checksum and

pages.summary: This page describes how to configure settings for ensuring message integrity with SWIFT using a specific screen in an application, including options for host code, local authentication, message direction, and key specification.
pages.raw_text:
2-14
ensure the integrity of messages exchanged with SWIFT. You can invoke this screen by 
typing вАШMSDLAUMNвАЩ in the top right corner of the Application toolbar and clicking the adjoining 
arrow button.
You can specify the following in this screen:
Host Code
Specify the host code. Alternatively, you can select the host code from the option list. The list 
displays all valid host codes maintained in the system.
Local Authentication Required
Check this box to enable SWIFT local authentication in the bank. If this check-box is checked 
then all other parameters in this screen is mandatory.
Message Direction
Choose the direction of the message based on the key maintained for Outgoing or Incoming 
SWIFT message.
Send Key
First Part
Specify the first part of the key. The length of the key must be in sixteen hexadecimal 
characters
Second Part
Specify the second part of the key. The length of the key must also be in sixteen hexadecimal 
characters.

pages.summary: This section explains how to view and search for SWIFT LAU maintenance details using the SWIFT LAU Summary screen.
pages.raw_text:
2-15
2.8.2 
Viewing SWIFT LAU Summary Details
You can view SWIFT LAU maintenance details in the SWIFT LAU Summary screen. You can 
invoke this screen by typing вАШMSSLAUMNвАЩ at the top right corner of the Application toolbar 
and clicking the adjoining arrow button.
You can search using one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†Authorization Status 
пБђпА†Record Status 
пБђпА†Host Code 
пБђпА†Message Direction 
Once you have specified the search parameters, click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The system displays 
the records that match the search criteria for the following:
пБђпА†Authorization Status 
пБђпА†Record Status 
пБђпА†Host Code 
пБђпА†Message Direction 
пБђпА†Local Authentication Required 
пБђпА†First Part 
пБђпА†Second Part

pages.summary: This page explains the workflow for generating and validating S-Block data in both outgoing and incoming SWIFT messages using Oracle FLEXCUBE and EMS modules.
pages.raw_text:
2-16
2.8.3 
Processing Outgoing SWIFT LAU
Outbound message workflow is explained below:
пБђпА†Oracle FLEXCUBE generates the S-Block for all outbound SWIFT messages with 
message status 'Generated'.
пБђпА†EMS module refers SWIFT LAU parameters in FLEXCUBE 'SWIFT LAU Maintenance' 
screen and applies HMAC-SHA256 algorithm to generate the checksum in S-Block for 
all outbound messages from FCUBS.
пБђпА†For all outbound messages, EMS-generated checksum is appended in the S-Block of 
the message, and the same is updated in the 'SWIFT LAU' field of Outgoing Message 
Browser screen.
пБђпА†Successful generation of S-Block data and message hand-off to out folder, will update 
the message status to 'Handoff'. If there is a failure in appending the S-Block data in the 
message, EMS will not update the message status to 'Handoff' in Outgoing Message 
Browser.
пБђпА†The next handoff job of EMS picks-up the messages with status 'Generated', 
regenerates S-Block data, appends the same in the message, and updates the 
message status to 'Handoff' in Outgoing Message Browser.
2.8.4 
Processing Incoming SWIFT LAU
Inbound message workflow is explained below:
пБђпА†You can validate inbound messages with the S-Block data. The checksum value is 
regenerated in EMS, based on SWIFT LAU parameters and HMAC-SHA256 algorithm.
пБђпА†Regenerated checksum is compared with the checksum present in the received 
message, and further processing is carried out in FCUBS based on data verification.
пБђпА†LAU checksum validated messages are uploaded into Incoming Message Browser with 
status 'Unprocessed', for FCUBS to create relevant contracts.

pages.summary: This page describes how checksum values are updated and validated in received messages, as well as details on invoking the Rule Group Mapping screen.
pages.raw_text:
2-17
пБђпА†The encrypted S-Block checksum values in the received messages are updated in the 
'SWIFT LAU' field, and the checksum generated by EMS for all incoming messages are 
updated in 'LAU Validation' field of Incoming Message Browser.
пБђпА†Messages that have failed in LAU checksum validation are uploaded into Incoming 
Message Browser with status 'Repair'.
пБђпА†Messages with status 'Repair' in Incoming Message Browser will hold differences in 
checksum data present in SWIFT LAU and LAU Validation fields.
пБђпА†Incoming messages without S Block is uploaded in incoming browser with status 
'Repair', and data is not updated in SWIFT LAU field, while EMS generated checksum 
is updated in LAU VALIDATION field.
2.9 
Mapping Rule Group
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.9.1, "Invoking Rule Group Mapping Screen"
пБђпА†Section 2.9.2, "Viewing Rule Group Mapping Summary"

Invoking Rule Group Mapping Screen
You can invoke вАШRule Group MappingвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDRLMAPвАЩ in the field at the top 
right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button..
You can specify the following fields:
Rule Group Name
Specify the unique rule group name.
Module ID
Specify the module that needs to be mapped to the created rule group.

2-18

2.13 File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration
You have to create below folder structure for the Swiftnet connectivity:

mstm_ems_system_parameters (APP_BASE_DIR)
2. Create below folder structure in the application base directory:
пБђпА†payload\EMS_OUT
пБђпА†xsl
3. In the Xsl folder copy all the xsl required for message transformation

sections: Invoking File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration Screen
Viewing File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration Summary
pages.summary: This page describes how to invoke and configure the File Transfer Adapter Connector screen for specifying various parameters related to file transfer operations.
pages.raw_text:
2-26
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.13.1, "Invoking File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration Screen"
пБђпА†Section 2.13.2, "Viewing File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration Summary"
2.13.1 
Invoking File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration Screen
You can invoke вАШFile Transfer Adapter Connector ConfigurationвАЩ screen by typing 
вАШMSDFTACNвАЩ in the field at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the 
adjoining arrow button...
You can specify the following fields:
Operation Type
Select the type of connectivity. Choose between Inbound and Outbound.
Host Code
Specify the host code.
Parameter File Required
Check this box if FCM creates or receives a Parameter File corresponding to each and every 
data file in configured file directory along with data file.
Data file LAU
Check this box if FCM calculates the LAU of Data File and puts the calculated value in 
parameter file.
Parameter File LAU
Check this box if parameters defined at emission profile of SAG side are overridden by 
information in companion file. 
Allow Overriding on SAG Profile
Check this box if parameters defined at the emission profile of SAG side will be overridden by 
information in Companion file. 
File Directory
Specifies store details of emission directory for outbound connectivity and reception directory 
for inbound flow. 
Log Directory
Specifies the responses from SAG.

sections: Viewing File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration Summary
pages.summary: This page describes various configuration settings and directories for file transfer operations, including success directory placement, debulk requirements, and media details. It also explains how to view the configuration summary of file transfer adapter connectors.
pages.raw_text:
2-27
Success Directory
For Outbound connectivity, Delivery notification response related to file transfer status from 
the SAG are placed in this directory. For inbound connectivity file transfer, success status files 
are placed in this folder.
Debulk Required
Check this box, if debulk is required for inbound messages.
Debulk Rule Name
Specifies the debulk rule name.
Node
Specifies the node details. This is mandatory for inbound.
Media
Specifies the media details. This is mandatory for inbound.
Media Control System
Specifies the media control system. This is mandatory for inbound.
2.13.2 
Viewing File Transfer Adapter Connector Configuration Summary
You can invoke вАЬFile Transfer Connector Configuration SummaryвАЭ screen by typing 
вАШMSSFTACNвАЩ in the field at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the 
adjoining arrow button. 
You can search using one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†FTA Connector Name 
пБђпА†Operation Mode
пБђпА†Auth Status 
пБђпА†Record Status

sections: Section 2.14.1 Invoking Distinguished Name Definition Screen
pages.summary: This page describes how to search for records based on specific parameters and provides details on invoking and viewing the distinguished name definition screen.
pages.raw_text:
2-28
Once you have specified the search parameters, click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The system displays 
the records that match the search criteria for the following:
пБђпА†FTA Connector Name 
пБђпА†Operation Mode
пБђпА†Auth Status 
пБђпА†Record Status
2.14 Distinguished Name Definition
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.14.1, "Invoking Distinguished Name Definition Screen"
пБђпА†Section 2.14.2, "Viewing Distinguished Name Summary"
2.14.1 
Invoking Distinguished Name Definition Screen
You can invoke вАШDistinguished Name DefinitionвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDDSTNMвАЩ in the field at 
the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button...
You can specify the following fields:
Distinguished Name ID
Specify the unique identification of the distinguished name.
Distinguished Name
Specify the distinguished name to identify the entity that sends or gets messages. 
Description
Specify the description of the distinguished name.

pages.summary: This page describes how to view a distinguished name summary by searching with specific parameters and introduces the process of defining debulk rules.
pages.raw_text:
2-29
2.14.2 
Viewing Distinguished Name Summary
You can invoke вАЬDistinguished Name SummaryвАЭ screen by typing вАШMSSDSTNMвАЩ in the field at 
the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button. 
You can search using one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†Distinguished Name Id
пБђпА†Distinguished Name
пБђпА†Authorization Status 
пБђпА†Record Status
Once you have specified the search parameters, click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The system displays 
the records that match the search criteria for the following:
пБђпА†Distinguished Name Id
пБђпА†Distinguished Name
пБђпА†Description
пБђпА†Authorization Status 
пБђпА†Record Status
2.15 Defining Debulk Rule
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.15.1, "Invoking Debulk Rule Definition Screen"
пБђпА†Section 2.15.2, "Viewing Debulk Rule Summary"

pages.summary: This section explains how to invoke and configure settings on the 'Debulk Rule Definition' screen for processing debulk files.
pages.raw_text:
2-30
2.15.1 
Invoking Debulk Rule Definition Screen
You can invoke вАШDebulk Rule DefinitionвАЩ screen by typing MSDDEBRLвАЩ in the field at the top 
right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button...
You can specify the following fields:
De bulk Rule Name
Specify the name of the debulk file.
Payload Delimiter
Specify the delimiter between payloads.
Protocol
Select the type of protocol.
Decompression Required
Check this box if debulk processing requires decompression.
Decompression Type
Select the type of decompression. Choose among the following:
пБђпА†ZIP
пБђпА†GZIP

pages.summary: This page describes how to access and search the 'Debulk Rule Summary' screen, as well as introduces the Connectivity Operation Manager section.
pages.raw_text:
2-31
2.15.2 
Viewing Debulk Rule Summary
You can invoke вАЬDebulk Rule SummaryвАЭ screen by typing вАШMSSDEBRLвАЩ in the field at the top 
right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button. 
You can search using one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†Authorization Status
пБђпА†Record Status
пБђпА†Debulk Rule Name
пБђпА†Protocol
Once you have specified the search parameters, click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The system displays 
the records that match the search criteria for the following:
пБђпА†Authorization Status
пБђпА†Record Status
пБђпА†Debulk Rule Name
пБђпА†Payload Delimiter
пБђпА†Protocol
2.16 Connectivity Operation Manager
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.16.1, "Invoking Connectivity Operation Manager Screen"

pages.summary: This section explains how to invoke and use the 'Connectivity Operation Manager' screen to manage connectivity lines.
pages.raw_text:
2-32
2.16.1 
Invoking Connectivity Operation Manager Screen
You can invoke вАШConnectivity Operation ManagerвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDCNMGRвАЩ in the field 
at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button...
You can specify the following fields:
Connectivity
Specify the required connectivity line.
Operation Type
Select the type of operation.
Status
Specify the display line status.
Start
Click the Start button to start the connectivity line.
Stop
Click the Stop button to start the connectivity line.

pages.summary: This section describes how to use the outbound file browser for searching and viewing outbound files based on various parameters and details that can be viewed or resent.
pages.raw_text:
2-33
2.17 Outbound File Browser
You can use the outbound file browser for searching and viewing the outbound files. To 
invoke the screen, type 'MSSFLBRW' in the field at the top right corner of the application 
toolbar and click the adjoining arrow button.
You can search for the records based on one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†File name of the bulked file
пБђпА†Bulk reference number
пБђпА†Generated date
пБђпА†Status 
пБђпА†Bulk preference 
Once you have specified the above details, click 'Execute Query' button. The system displays 
the records that match the search criteria. You can double-click a record to view the details in 
the 'Outbound File Browser Details' (MSDFLBRW) screen. 
You can view the following details in this screen.
пБђпА†File name
пБђпА†Bulk reference number
пБђпА†File ID
пБђпА†Generated date 
пБђпА†Generated time
пБђпА†Status
пБђпА†Bulk preference
пБђпА†Number of transactions
пБђпА†File size
You can resend the selected file from this screen using the 'Resend' button.

pages.summary: This section describes how to use the inbound file browser for searching and viewing inbound files based on various parameters such as file name, received date, status, and more.
pages.raw_text:
2-34
2.18 Inbound File Browser
You can use the inbound file browser for searching and viewing the inbound files. To invoke 
the screen, type 'MSSIFBRW' in the field at the top right corner of the application toolbar and 
click the adjoining arrow button.
You can search for the records based on one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†File name
пБђпА†Received date
пБђпА†Status
пБђпА†Logical file name
пБђпА†Transaction ID
пБђпА†File Act connector name
Once you have specified the above details, click 'Execute Query' button. The system displays 
the following details of records that match the search criteria. 
пБђпА†File name
пБђпА†Received Date
пБђпА†Received Time
пБђпА†Status
пБђпА†File Size
пБђпА†Logical File Name
пБђпА†Transaction Id
пБђпА†FileAct Connector Name
пБђпА†Error Code
пБђпА†Error Parameter
пБђпА†Error Description

pages.summary: This page describes how to define bulk preference settings and invoke the screen for doing so, including fields like name, trigger types, and scheduler type.
pages.raw_text:
2-35
2.19 Defining Bulk Preference
This section contains the following topic:
пБђпА†Section 2.19.1, "Invoking Bulk Preference Definition Screen"
пБђпА†Section 2.19.2, "Viewing Bulking Preference Definition Summary"
2.19.1 
Invoking Bulk Preference Definition Screen
You can invoke вАШBulk Preference DefinitionвАЩ screen by typing вАШMSDBLKRLвАЩ in the field at the 
top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow button...
You can specify the following fields:
Bulk Preference Name
Specify the name used for storing the Bulk Preference.
Volume Based Trigger
Check this box to configure volume based bulking.
Maximum number of messages
Specify the maximum number of messages that can be stored in a bulk file.
Minimum number of messages
Specify the maximum number of messages that can be stored in a bulk file.
Maximum size in KB
Specify the distinguished name to identify the entity that sends or gets messages.
Minimum size in KB
Specify the distinguished name to identify the entity that sends or gets messages.
Size based Trigger
Check this field to configure size based bulking.
Scheduler Type
Select the type of scheduler. Choose any one of the following:
пБђпА†Time Based

pages.summary: This page details various settings and parameters for scheduling and file management, including time intervals, file formats, and destination types.
pages.raw_text:
2-36
пБђпА†Frequency 
Start Time
Specify the Start Time of the trigger. Enter the format in HH:MM format.
End Time
Specify the Start Time of the trigger. Enter the format in HH:MM format.
Time Interval in Min
Specify the interval of the repeat in MM format.
Scheduling No
Specify the scheduling sequence.
Start Hour
Specify the start hour of the scheduling sequence.
Start Min
Specify the start minute for frequency based scheduler.
Bulk File Name Prefix
Specify the prefix to indicate the name of the bulk file.
Maximum size of a file of KB
Specify the maximum size of a bulk file in KB.
Payload Delimiter
Specify the delimiter while bulking messages.
Maximum settlement amount for a file
Specify the maximum settlement for a file that must be considered while bulking messages.
File Format
Select the file that must be used while bulking messages.
File Type
Select the file type that must be used while bulking messages.
Compression Required
Check this box to indicate that file compression is required.
Compression Type
Specify the required type of compression.
Destination Type
Specify the type of destination.
пБђпА†Folder
пБђпА†Queue
пБђпА†SwiftNet Connectivity
If destination is Swiftnet connectivity then a field swiftnet connectivity needs to be selected 
from LOV which gives the list of connectivity lines.
Folder Path
Specify the user defined destination folder path.

pages.summary: This page describes settings for queue JNDI name, protocol type selection, and how to view bulking preference definition summary in a specific application.
pages.raw_text:
2-37
Queue JNDI Name
Specify JNDI name of the Queue if the destination type is Queue.
SwiftNet Connectivity
Select the swiftnet connectivity from the option list.
Protocol Type
Select the type of Protocol.
пБђпА†FTA
Protocol Name
Specify the protocol name required for FTA protocol.
2.19.2 
Viewing Bulking Preference Definition Summary
You can invoke вАЬBulking Preference Definition SummaryвАЭ screen by typing вАШMSSBLKRLвАЩ in 
the field at the top right corner of the Application tool bar and clicking on the adjoining arrow 
button. 
You can search using one or more of the following parameters:
пБђпА†Bulking Preference Name 
пБђпА†Bulk Initiated
пБђпА†Auth Initiated 
пБђпА†Record Status
Once you have specified the search parameters, click вАШSearchвАЩ button. The system displays 
the records that match the search criteria for the following:
пБђпА†Bulking Preference Name
пБђпА†Frequency
пБђпА†Max Number of Messages
пБђпА†Size (in KB)

pages.summary: This page describes how to use the 'Bulk Monitor' screen to search and monitor bulk messages and executions based on various parameters.
pages.raw_text:
2-38
пБђпА†File Format
пБђпА†Bulk Filename Prefix
пБђпА†Maximum size of bulk file (in KB)
пБђпА†Bulk Initiated
пБђпА†Destination Type
пБђпА†Auth Status
пБђпА†Record Status
2.20 Bulk Monitor
You can monitor the messages and executions in bulk using 'Bulk Monitor' screen. To invoke 
this screen, type 'MSSBLKMN' in the field at the top right corner of the application toolbar and 
click the adjoining arrow button.
You can search for the records based on one or more of the following parameters.
пБђпА†Execution start time
пБђпА†Execution end time
пБђпА†Bulk preference
пБђпА†Status
пБђпА†Number of files generated
Once you have specified the search parameters, click 'Execute Query' button. The system 
displays the following details of the records that match the filter criteria.

pages.summary: This page describes how to view and manage details of records in the 'Bulk Monitor Detail' screen, including options to regenerate files or initiate ad-hoc bulk processing.
pages.raw_text:
2-39
You can view the details of each record in the results. Double-click a record from the results 
to view the details in 'Bulk Monitor Detail' (MSDBLKMN) screen. 
This screen displays the following details of the record.
пБђпА†Process reference number
пБђпА†Execution start date
пБђпА†Execution start time
пБђпА†Execution end date
пБђпА†Execution end time
пБђпА†Status
пБђпА†Bulk preference name
пБђпА†Number of files generated
пБђпА†Error code
пБђпА†Error parameter
пБђпА†Error description
You can regenerate the files by clicking 'Regenerate' button. You can also bulk a selected bulk 
preference ad-hoc using 'Initiate Adhoc Bulking' button.

pages.summary: This page lists various Function IDs and their corresponding page numbers in a glossary.
